rag_rules: When AGREE diagnostics report unresolved references or type errors, trace the root cause to invalid feature names or incorrect types in the same line and fix or remove the offending clause.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE contracts; avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers not present in the model.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, ensure all expressions have consistent types and all referenced identifiers are resolvable within the current scope.
Place implementation-level assignments in implementation scopes and type-level assumptions/guarantees in type scopes.
Avoid introducing new data types or package references that are not already present in the AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use `eq` for expressions and `gua` for guarantees; avoid placing `gua` in implementation scopes.
Constants and equations in AGREE must declare valid scalar types: int, real, or bool.
anti_patterns: Do not add new package-qualified identifiers or data types not defined in the AADL model.
Do not place guarantees in implementation scopes; use `assign` for output bindings instead.
